title: Russian Hill-Paris Block Architectural District - Wikipedia
content: The Russian Hill-Paris Block Architectural District is a 1.5-acre (0.61 ha) historic district located in the Russian Hill area of San Francisco, California

title: National Register #87002288: Russian Hill Paris Block Architectural District in San Francisco
content: Paris Block c1869, Courtesy California Historical Society, San Francisco) Paris Block c1869. Green Hill Tower) Green Hill Tower at 1070 Green Street. Russian Hill/Paris Block Architectural District. The Paris Block Architectural District is a group of eleven dwellings facing each other on a single, flat block atop Russian Hill. The 1906 Earthquake and Fire destroyed San Francisco except for the western and southern suburbs and a few pockets such as Russian Hill where many dwellings were saved by residents who refused the order to leave and by the sparseness of development on the hilltop. The name Paris Block was probably coined by Margot Patterson Doss in a column titled "Explore the Diversities of the Paris Block," *San Francisco Chronicle*, 5 July 1964. This Pueblo/Mission Revival residence was designed by architect Oscar Haupt who is best known for Altenheim in Oakland, Winfield Scott Elementary School in San Francisco and the top two floors of the Baldwin Hotel in San Francisco.
